source_pr.title: expression, types: freeze unsafe datetime warning args

symptoms: `TestIssue48741` was flaky because it polls for a GC triggered by memory-limit changes, but GC timing depends on runtime/CI scheduling and may not run promptly within the polling window.
failure_signature: `require.Eventually` timed out waiting for GC-related state changes in `TestIssue48741`.
root_cause_explanation: The gctuner test was timing-sensitive because it waited for runtime GC to be triggered by memory-limit adjustments; depending on scheduler/CI load, GC might not happen quickly enough and the Eventually loop could time out. The PR calls `runtime.GC()` inside the polling predicate to force the GC progress and make the condition deterministic.
fix_pattern: Force GC progress during polling by calling `runtime.GC()` inside the `Eventually` predicate in `TestIssue48741`, reducing dependence on runtime scheduling.

evidence: evidence: add `runtime.GC()` inside `Eventually` predicate to stabilize polling for GC-triggered state changes.
